title: haplopappus nelsonii
intro: Haplopappus nelsonii, also known as Nelson's goldenweed, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and digestive health. Haplopappus nelsonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
